_comment: templates/examples/suppressions.json -- per-finding-hash allowlist (W691 substrate). Copy to .roam/suppressions.json at your repo root. Reach this file via `roam suppress <finding_id>` after manually verifying a finding is a false positive. The math / over-fetch / missing-index / auth-gaps detectors flag the matched finding with `suppressed: {source: 'suppressions.json', ...}` instead of dropping it. The SARIF projection at roam.output.sarif._load_suppressions consumes the same file via the optional rule_id + location fields.
_schema_reference: Canonical loader: roam.commands.finding_suppress._load_per_finding_suppressions. Typed loader: roam.commands.finding_suppress.load_per_finding_suppressions_typed. Dataclass: roam.policy.suppression_v2.FindingIdSuppression. SARIF projection: roam.output.sarif._load_suppressions. Required key: top-level finding_id (16-char sha256 prefix from roam.commands.finding_suppress.finding_id). Required entry field: reason. Optional entry fields: added_at, rule_id, location, task_id, symbol_name, author, sarif_status (suppressed/notSuppressed), policy_status (accepted/dismissed/accepted_with_caveats), expires. NOTE: the legacy 'status' field (safe/acknowledged/wont-fix) is deprecated per W744; use sarif_status AND/OR policy_status instead.
